---
title: "Newsroom Analytics Dashboard"
output:
  flexdashboard::flex_dashboard:
    orientation: columns
    vertical_layout: fill
    theme: bootstrap
    source_code: embed
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
library(flexdashboard)
library(tidyverse)
library(plotly)
library(DT)

# Load data
articles <- read_csv("data/raw/articles.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)
users <- read_csv("data/raw/users.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)
sessions <- read_csv("data/raw/sessions.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)
article_events <- read_csv("data/raw/article_events.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)
ab_test_events <- read_csv("data/raw/ab_test_events.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)

# Prepare engagement data
engagement_data <- article_events %>%
  left_join(articles, by = "article_id") %>%
  left_join(sessions, by = "session_id")
```

Overview
=======================================================================

Column {data-width=150}
-----------------------------------------------------------------------

### Total Pageviews
```{r}
valueBox(
  nrow(article_events),
  icon = "fa-eye",
  color = "primary"
)
```

### Engaged Reads
```{r}
valueBox(
  sum(article_events$engaged_read),
  icon = "fa-thumbs-up",
  color = "success"
)
```

### Subscriptions
```{r}
valueBox(
  sum(article_events$subscribed),
  icon = "fa-star",
  color = "warning"
)
```

### Avg Engagement Rate
```{r}
rate <- mean(article_events$engaged_read) * 100
valueBox(
  sprintf("%.1f%%", rate),
  icon = "fa-percent",
  color = "info"
)
```

Column {data-width=350}
-----------------------------------------------------------------------

### Daily Engagement Trend
```{r}
daily_trend <- engagement_data %>%
  mutate(date = as.Date(event_timestamp)) %>%
  group_by(date) %>%
  summarise(
    pageviews = n(),
    engagement_rate = mean(engaged_read)
  ) %>%
  arrange(date)

plot_ly(daily_trend, x = ~date, y = ~engagement_rate, type = 'scatter', mode = 'lines+markers') %>%
  layout(
    title = "Daily Engagement Rate",
    xaxis = list(title = "Date"),
    yaxis = list(title = "Engagement Rate", tickformat = ".1%")
  )
```

### Traffic Source Distribution
```{r}
traffic <- sessions %>%
  count(traffic_source) %>%
  mutate(percentage = n / sum(n) * 100)

plot_ly(traffic, labels = ~traffic_source, values = ~n, type = 'pie') %>%
  layout(title = "Traffic Source Distribution")
```

Article Performance
=======================================================================

Column {data-width=400}
-----------------------------------------------------------------------

### Top Articles by Engagement
```{r}
top_articles <- engagement_data %>%
  group_by(title, section) %>%
  summarise(
    views = n(),
    engagement_rate = mean(engaged_read),
    avg_time = mean(time_on_page_seconds)
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(engagement_rate)) %>%
  head(10)

plot_ly(top_articles, x = ~engagement_rate, y = ~reorder(title, engagement_rate),
        type = 'bar', orientation = 'h',
        text = ~sprintf("%.1f%% engagement<br>%d views", engagement_rate*100, views),
        hoverinfo = 'text') %>%
  layout(
    title = "Top 10 Articles by Engagement",
    xaxis = list(title = "Engagement Rate", tickformat = ".1%"),
    yaxis = list(title = "")
  )
```

Column {data-width=300}
-----------------------------------------------------------------------

### Engagement by Section
```{r}
section_perf <- engagement_data %>%
  group_by(section) %>%
  summarise(
    avg_engagement = mean(engaged_read),
    total_views = n()
  )

plot_ly(section_perf, x = ~section, y = ~avg_engagement, type = 'bar',
        text = ~sprintf("%.1f%%<br>%d views", avg_engagement*100, total_views),
        hoverinfo = 'text') %>%
  layout(
    title = "Engagement by Section",
    xaxis = list(title = "Section"),
    yaxis = list(title = "Avg Engagement", tickformat = ".1%")
  )
```

### Article Details Table
```{r}
article_summary <- engagement_data %>%
  group_by(article_id, title, section, author) %>%
  summarise(
    views = n(),
    avg_time = mean(time_on_page_seconds),
    engagement_rate = mean(engaged_read),
    subscriptions = sum(subscribed)
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(views))

datatable(
  article_summary,
  options = list(pageLength = 10, scrollX = TRUE),
  colnames = c("ID", "Title", "Section", "Author", "Views", "Avg Time", "Engagement", "Subscriptions")
) %>%
  formatPercentage("engagement_rate", 1) %>%
  formatRound("avg_time", 1)
```

Audience Behavior
=======================================================================

Column {data-width=300}
-----------------------------------------------------------------------

### Device Type Comparison
```{r}
device_comp <- engagement_data %>%
  group_by(device_type) %>%
  summarise(
    sessions = n_distinct(session_id),
    avg_engagement = mean(engaged_read),
    avg_time = mean(time_on_page_seconds),
    avg_scroll = mean(scroll_depth_pct)
  )

plot_ly(device_comp, x = ~device_type, y = ~sessions, type = 'bar', name = 'Sessions') %>%
  add_trace(y = ~avg_engagement, name = 'Engagement Rate', yaxis = 'y2') %>%
  layout(
    title = "Device Type Analysis",
    xaxis = list(title = "Device Type"),
    yaxis = list(title = "Sessions"),
    yaxis2 = list(title = "Engagement Rate", overlaying = "y", side = "right", tickformat = ".1%")
  )
```

Column {data-width=400}
-----------------------------------------------------------------------

### Engagement Metrics by Device
```{r}
device_metrics <- engagement_data %>%
  group_by(device_type) %>%
  summarise(
    avg_time = mean(time_on_page_seconds),
    avg_scroll = mean(scroll_depth_pct),
    engagement_rate = mean(engaged_read),
    subscription_rate = mean(subscribed)
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(avg_time, avg_scroll, engagement_rate, subscription_rate),
               names_to = "metric", values_to = "value")

plot_ly(device_metrics, x = ~device_type, y = ~value, color = ~metric, type = 'bar') %>%
  layout(
    title = "Engagement Metrics by Device",
    xaxis = list(title = "Device Type"),
    yaxis = list(title = "Value"),
    barmode = 'group'
  )
```

### User Journey Analysis
```{r}
user_journey <- engagement_data %>%
  group_by(session_id) %>%
  summarise(
    pageviews = n(),
    engaged_reads = sum(engaged_read),
    total_time = sum(time_on_page_seconds),
    max_scroll = max(scroll_depth_pct),
    subscribed = max(subscribed)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    engagement_rate = engaged_reads / pageviews,
    avg_time_per_page = total_time / pageviews
  )

# Scatter plot of engagement vs pageviews
plot_ly(user_journey, x = ~pageviews, y = ~engagement_rate,
        type = 'scatter', mode = 'markers',
        size = ~avg_time_per_page, color = ~subscribed,
        text = ~sprintf("Session: %s<br>Pageviews: %d<br>Engagement: %.1f%%",
                       session_id, pageviews, engagement_rate*100),
        hoverinfo = 'text') %>%
  layout(
    title = "User Journey: Pageviews vs Engagement",
    xaxis = list(title = "Pageviews per Session"),
    yaxis = list(title = "Engagement Rate", tickformat = ".1%")
  )
```

A/B Testing
=======================================================================

Column {data-width=250}
-----------------------------------------------------------------------

### A/B Test Overview
```{r}
ab_summary <- ab_test_events %>%
  group_by(variant) %>%
  summarise(
    impressions = sum(impression),
    clicks = sum(click),
    ctr = clicks / impressions,
    engaged_reads = sum(engaged_read),
    subscriptions = sum(subscription)
  )

valueBox(
  sprintf("%.1f%%", ab_summary$ctr[ab_summary$variant == "A"] * 100),
  "CTR Variant A",
  icon = "fa-mouse-pointer",
  color = "primary"
)

valueBox(
  sprintf("%.1f%%", ab_summary$ctr[ab_summary$variant == "B"] * 100),
  "CTR Variant B",
  icon = "fa-mouse-pointer",
  color = "success"
)
```

### Statistical Significance
```{r}
test <- prop.test(
  x = c(ab_summary$clicks[ab_summary$variant == "A"], ab_summary$clicks[ab_summary$variant == "B"]),
  n = c(ab_summary$impressions[ab_summary$variant == "A"], ab_summary$impressions[ab_summary$variant == "B"])
)

significance <- ifelse(test$p.value < 0.05, "Significant", "Not Significant")
color <- ifelse(test$p.value < 0.05, "success", "warning")

valueBox(
  significance,
  paste("p =", format.pval(test$p.value, digits = 3)),
  icon = "fa-chart-line",
  color = color
)
```

Column {data-width=500}
-----------------------------------------------------------------------

### A/B Test Results
```{r}
ab_long <- ab_summary %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(ctr, engaged_reads, subscriptions),
               names_to = "metric", values_to = "value") %>%
  mutate(metric = case_when(
    metric == "ctr" ~ "Click-Through Rate",
    metric == "engaged_reads" ~ "Engaged Reads",
    metric == "subscriptions" ~ "Subscriptions"
  ))

plot_ly(ab_long, x = ~variant, y = ~value, color = ~metric, type = 'bar') %>%
  layout(
    title = "A/B Test Results by Metric",
    xaxis = list(title = "Variant"),
    yaxis = list(title = "Value"),
    barmode = 'group'
  )
```

### Test Details
```{r}
ab_detailed <- ab_test_events %>%
  group_by(variant) %>%
  summarise(
    impressions = sum(impression),
    clicks = sum(click),
    ctr = clicks / impressions,
    engaged_read_rate = sum(engaged_read) / impressions,
    subscription_rate = sum(subscription) / impressions
  ) %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~sprintf("%.3f", .)))

datatable(
  ab_detailed,
  options = list(dom = 't', paging = FALSE),
  colnames = c("Variant", "Impressions", "Clicks", "CTR", "Engaged Read Rate", "Subscription Rate")
)
```
